content.full_text:
October 8, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi  
Technical Analyst, California  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95814-0807

Re: Confirmation Of Telephone Advice

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This firm serves as legal counsel to Taxpayers For Common Sense, a ballot measure committee organized for the purpose of opposing Proposition 134. On behalf of this committee, I am writing to confirm telephone advice I received from you on Friday, October 5, with regard to application of the California "sponsored committee" requirements to broadcast advertisements.

As you recall, I explained that my client intends to begin airing broadcast advertisements which recommend both a "yes" vote on one measure and a "no" vote on another measure. Specifically, Taxpayers for Common Sense intends to recommend that voters vote "yes" on Proposition 126 and "no" on Proposition 134. Because the advertisement's message is not "more than fifty percent devoted to one initiative," the advertisements are exempt from the provisions of Proposition 105 (Government Code Sections 84501 et seq.).

I then inquired whether the "sponsored committee" identification requirements of Government Code Section 84106 were applicable to broadcast advertisements. You indicated that while identification of a committee's "sponsor" is required in statements of organization, campaign reports, and on mass mailings, the FPPC has never opined that sponsorship identification is required on broadcast advertisements. You further indicated that the subject of broadcast advertisement disclaimers does not fall within the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Mr. Wayne Imberi  
October 8, 1990  
Page Two

Thank you for your prompt response to my inquiry. I have advised my client accordingly, and they have begun production of advertisements which will soon begin appearing on California television and radio stations. Therefore, in the event that I have inadvertently misstated or misunderstood any of your advice, please contact me at the earliest possible time.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Wes Van Winkle  
Of Counsel

wvw/jjm  
mtcs:10-8ltr
